canonical_question: How do excess features and changes activate organizational antibodies?
constraints: Applies to organizations large enough to have formal review processes (typically 200+ employees with established IT, legal, and compliance functions) — small teams without institutional antibodies will not exhibit this pattern
The principle identifies the trigger mechanism but does not prescribe the organizational restructuring needed to reduce antibody count — that requires separate change management methodology

Antigen surface area is only one variable in deal or adoption failure — structural misfit (wrong problem-solution match) and political misalignment (no consensus among decision-makers) are independent failure modes [src1]
Minimizing surface area can be taken too far — stripping essential features to reduce resistance may produce a solution too limited to deliver value, creating a different type of rejection [src3]
Requires understanding of the specific organization's immune topology — what triggers antibodies in one company (security review) may be dormant in another

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs the full organizational immune system theory, not just the antigen mechanism
User needs to assess how much friction is appropriate rather than minimize surface area
User needs to catalog which specific antibody types exist in an organization
